text: Hi, welcome to This is My Architecture New Zealand. I'm Shalak and we have Martin from Matter today.
Thanks for having me. Martin, why don't we start with telling us about Matter?
Yes, certainly. Matter is a New Zealand based company and Matter specializes in
digital trust and verifiable data. We enable our customers to build global solutions in
the verifiable credential and digital identity space. We also are engaged in
standard committees in order to help build and shape these standards.
Right. Can you tell us about a use case, please?
Yes. So we've recently developed a verification capability for the New Zealand My Vaccine Pass,
which we refer to as the Verifier API. So a user can actually get their credentials
pre-verified by larger entities, for example, a workplace or a university or before an event.
Right. So having that credential is very important. What happens just before the event?
Yes. So for example, let's use the example of a fitness club. So a fitness club user wants to use
his credentials and show his credentials to the club. So the club collects the credential from the user
beforehand and sends these credentials to our infrastructure. That can be either in the form of a
screenshot, a PDF or a photo. So this hits our infrastructure in a request. We verify this request
using WAF and Shield Advanced in order to do sanity checks on the request. We're looking for certain
headers. We drop some headers and we look for a certain content length.
Security is first and foremost. I really noticed here you are using Shield Advanced. So where is Shield
Basic comes for free?
So we decided to use Shield Advanced because that gives us access to the Shield team. And they not only helped us
in advance to configure Shield in a way that suits our needs, but also they can help us during drills and they can
they do rapid response whenever we might be under attack.
Yeah. Shield response team, free drills and rapid response. That's all about Shield Advanced.
So talking about scale and event, you might be dealing with a lot of uncertainty of users. How did you make sure those
potential users are not flagged as DDoS?
Yeah, that's a good point actually, because when we launched the services, it obviously had quite some
popularity. And so we had to make sure that the initial peak load was not flagged as a DDoS attack. So we
carefully had to configure Shield and WAF in a way so that the traffic was interpreted in the right way.
Right. And when you talk about traffic, did you have to scale the infrastructure?
Certainly. So once the request hits our infrastructure, our microservices, which we deploy within EKS
and within an auto scaling group, we had to prime this infrastructure in order to handle a multitude of the
requests that we were expecting. So we scaled it out. And then after the initial peak load, we verified multiple
hundred thousands of passes within the first week. We were able to scale it back in, in order to
accommodate normal workload. That's cool. It is really good how you show in that you were able to scale
out and scale back in during a peak event. And did the auto scaling helped it?
Yes, certainly. So that obviously takes the pressure out of us to manually scale out and in.
Cool. And that is a really good use case of how did you cost optimize as well. Did you have any design
principles while building this whole solution? Yes. So we use Terraform as infrastructure as well.
And then we use Terraform as code. That helps us to keep our agility and in order to reproduce these
environments in multiple stages. And we can be sure that whenever we build this environment, it looks
exactly the same. Yeah. Terraform and infrastructure as code. That is so cool. It is great to see how
Matter is using AWS to build a verifiable credentials and supporting lives of Kiwis.
Thanks for having me. Thanks for watching another episode of This is My Architecture.
This is my Architecture.
